race.name: Jatkankynttila Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers through the Lapland wilderness near Rovaniemi — named after the iconic Lumberjack's Candle bridge, a Finnish mass skiing event with Arctic reliability.

climate.description: March in Rovaniemi at the Arctic Circle means reliable snow and lengthening days. Temperatures range from -15°C to 0°C. The Arctic position ensures consistent snow well into April. March brings increasing daylight and warmth — spring skiing conditions with firm morning snow.
challenges: Arctic cold — temperatures can still drop well below -10°C in March
Flat Lapland terrain means wind exposure
Spring sun can create icy morning and soft afternoon conditions
Remote Arctic location — limited international accessibility

course.primary: A 50km course through the Lapland wilderness around Rovaniemi, near the Arctic Circle. Flat to gently rolling terrain through boreal forest and along frozen rivers. Named after the iconic Jatkankynttila (Lumberjack's Candle) bridge, a landmark of Rovaniemi.
course.surface: Machine-groomed Lapland trails through boreal forest
features: Arctic Circle location — reliable March snow conditions
Named after the iconic Jatkankynttila bridge
Boreal forest and frozen river terrain
Rovaniemi — the 'Official Hometown of Santa Claus'
Both classic and freestyle techniques offered

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum snow reliability at the Arctic Circle. Arctic Circle scenery with the iconic Lumberjack's Candle bridge, frozen rivers, and boreal forest elevate the visual experience. Flat Lapland terrain limits elevation and technicality. Small field and limited international visibility keep prestige low.

history.summary: The Jatkankynttila Ski Marathon is a Lapland mass skiing event based in Rovaniemi, the capital of Finnish Lapland at the Arctic Circle. Named after the iconic Jatkankynttila (Lumberjack's Candle) bridge — a modern landmark that has become a symbol of the city — the race takes advantage of Lapland's guaranteed March snow conditions. It represents the deep Finnish massahiihto tradition extended to the Arctic north.
notable_facts: Named after the Jatkankynttila (Lumberjack's Candle) bridge in Rovaniemi
Arctic Circle location — among the northernmost ski marathons in the world
Rovaniemi is the official capital of Finnish Lapland
Reliable March snow conditions guaranteed by Arctic latitude
Part of Finnish massahiihto (mass skiing) culture
Both classic and freestyle techniques offered
